text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little village, there was a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver spent his days perched atop the largest oak in the meadow, observing the world with hawk-like attention.

One autumn day, as the first leaves began to fall, Oliver heard a peculiar humming coming from a nearby attic. Intrigued, he fluttered his way up through the loose plaster until he discovered a young girl struggling to lift a heavy bookshelf.

"We've lost our treasure," she lamented, her voice quivering.

Oliver listened intently and offered a wise old owl's guiding insight. "Have you tried reaching up?"

She nodded. "Where?"

"Up there." With that, Oliver flew up and guided her hand. She lifted the shelf just enough, then set down a beautifully bound book. "Oh! This is priceless!"

She hugged the book tightly. "You saved it! That's my family diary!"

"Well," said Oliver thoughtfully, "that diary
